questions: Q1: Was the canonical content module created correctly with all required sections?
Q2: Was the package.json exports map added correctly?
Q3: Does the compiled output pass all quality gates?

answers.answer: Yes. src/docs/cli-content.ts was created as a pure-data module (zero import statements) exporting cliDocsContent with id, title, content (markdown), order, and keywords. Content includes all required sections: Installation (npm install -g @projectxinnovation/helix-cli@latest), Setup & Authentication (hlx login, --manual, token add), Common Commands (tickets, inspect, comments, update with all flags), Worked Examples (8 examples covering all 6 required commands), Updating (hlx update, --enable-auto, --disable-auto), and Troubleshooting (stale symlink, auth issues, config location). All flags verified against source code.
evidence: src/docs/cli-content.ts: zero import statements (grep confirms), exports cliDocsContent with correct shape
Content verification: all 14 required checks passed (install cmd, update cmd, 6 command examples, and structural fields)

answers.answer: Yes. package.json exports field added with './docs' entry containing types (./dist/docs/cli-content.d.ts) and import (./dist/docs/cli-content.js) conditions. types condition placed first per TypeScript convention. Only import condition used (ESM-only package). No other package.json fields changed.
evidence: package.json lines 9-14: exports field with correct structure
No changes to files, scripts, bin, or any other field

answers.answer: Yes. npm run build exits 0, producing dist/docs/cli-content.js (8063 bytes) and dist/docs/cli-content.d.ts (141 bytes). npm run typecheck exits 0. No Node.js built-in references in compiled output (grep confirms). The compiled module can be dynamically imported and contains all expected exports.
evidence: ls dist/docs/: cli-content.js (8063 bytes), cli-content.d.ts (141 bytes)
No require() or Node built-in imports in compiled output

statement: Created src/docs/cli-content.ts as a zero-import pure-data TypeScript module exporting canonical CLI documentation content, and added package.json exports map at './docs'. The content covers install, auth/setup, command groups with all verified flags, 8 worked examples (covering all 6 required commands), update instructions, and troubleshooting. Build and typecheck pass. Compiled output is browser-safe with no Node.js dependencies.
